reasoning: By mid-to-late 2025, scientific agencies including NOAA and Berkeley Earth, as well as tracking by Carbon Brief, confirmed that 2025 was projected to be the second or third warmest year on record, falling short of the massive records set in 2023 and 2024. In August 2025, NOAA gave a less than 1% chance that 2025 would be the warmest year on record. By November 2025, publications continued to note that 2025 was set to be the second or third hottest year. Consequently, it is virtually impossible for 2025 to end up as the hottest year on record in the final NASA GISTEMP dataset.
